title: Local government administrative occupations
description: Examine, evaluate, and investigate eligibility for or conformity with laws and regulations governing contract compliance of licenses and permits, and perform other compliance and enforcement inspection and analysis activities not classified elsewhere. Inspect and investigate sources of pollution to protect the public and environment and ensure conformance with Federal, State, and local regulations and ordinances. Monitor and evaluate compliance with equal opportunity laws, guidelines, and policies to ensure that employment practices and contracting arrangements give equal opportunity without regard to race, religion, color, national origin, sex, age, or disability. (Based on 30 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Collect and analyze data, such as studying records or participating in population surveys.
Ask questions to obtain specified information, such as name, address, age, or state of residency.
Compile, record, and code results or data from interviews or surveys using a computer or specified form.
Review data obtained from interviews for completeness and accuracy and ensure validity of data.
Prepare reports and correspondence in response to specific problems or findings, including inspection results.
Examine permits, licenses, and applications to ensure compliance with regulations and licensing requirements.
Investigate employment practices or alleged violations of laws to document and correct discriminatory factors.
Monitor investigations of suspected offenders to ensure compliance with constitutional requirements and ethical standards.
Determine which compliance and enforcement activities to coordinate with other government agencies based on violation reports.
Verify that hazardous chemicals are handled, stored, and disposed of according to regulations.
Consult with community representatives to develop technical assistance agreements in accordance with governmental regulations.
Prepare reports of selection, survey, or other statistics and recommendations for corrective action.
Provide information, technical assistance, or training on employee supervision, hiring, grievance procedures, or staff development.
Review contracts and documents to ensure compliance with equal opportunity provisions and governmental regulations.
Determine sampling locations and methods, and collect water or wastewater samples for analysis, preserving samples properly.
Prepare, organize, and maintain inspection records and reports summarizing requirements and regulations, including enforcement documentation.
Investigate complaints regarding illegal dumping, pollution, or other suspected violations, and interview individuals to obtain evidence.
Monitor follow-up actions from identified violations and review compliance monitoring reports.
Inspect government properties such as construction sites for compliance with contract specifications or legal requirements.
Classify goods according to tariff coding systems and advise customers on import and export restrictions or customs-related matters.
Coordinate activities with management and other personnel to resolve problems and ensure compliance with departmental policies and procedures.
Compile, record, and evaluate data to verify completeness and determine eligibility status for public assistance applications.
Evaluate applications, records, or documents to gather information about eligibility or liability issues and initiate related procedures.
Monitor implementation of guidelines for nondiscriminatory employment practices and develop related materials.
Train or instruct employees in job duties or arrange for training to be provided according to company policies.
Complete and file death certificates and relevant paperwork according to legal requirements and procedures.
Inquire into circumstances of deaths, document evidence, and interview witnesses to understand relevant details.
Participate in audits, ensure compliance with regulations, and prepare necessary documents for regulatory agency submissions.
Develop or update procedures, policies, or standards based on changes in regulations or operational needs.
Resolve customer or applicant complaints and answer questions regarding policies and procedures effectively.
Testify at court hearings and provide transcripts or documentation as requested by legal authorities or the public.
File a legible transcript of records of a court case with the court clerk's office and file and store shorthand notes of the court session.
Ask speakers to clarify inaudible statements and transcribe recorded proceedings in accordance with established formats.
Edit, proofread, and type correspondence and other documents from notes or dictating machines using computers or typewriters.
Open, sort, and distribute incoming correspondence, including faxes and emails, and route incoming mail and answer correspondence.
Conduct research, compile data, and prepare papers for consideration and presentation by executives, committees, and boards of directors.
Coordinate office services, such as records, finances, budget preparation, personnel issues, and housekeeping, to assist executives.
Prepare responses to correspondence containing routine inquiries and prepare, maintain, and review purchasing files, reports, and price lists.
Prepare agendas and make arrangements for committee, board, and other meetings, and compile, transcribe, and distribute minutes of meetings.
Approve and pay bills, and calculate costs of orders, including charging or forwarding invoices to appropriate accounts.
Perform clerical tasks, such as answering phones or distributing mail and maintain knowledge of organizational and governmental rules affecting purchases.
Monitor contractor performance, recommending contract modifications when necessary and verify that transportation and handling procedures meet regulatory requirements.
Inspect food products and processing procedures for safety and compliance with regulations, and monitor the grading performed by employees to verify conformity to standards.
Review files, records, and other documents to obtain information to respond to requests, and maintain and update filing and database systems.
Compile, copy, sort, and file records of office activities, business transactions, and other activities, and compute, record, and proofread data and other information.
Collect, count, and disburse money, perform basic bookkeeping, and complete banking transactions as required.
Answer customer inquiries, provide information about services, and assist with loans and transactions, including presenting loan and repayment schedules.
Interview loan applicants to obtain personal and financial data and review and verify data for accuracy on loan application and closing documents.
Submit loan applications for underwriting approval and file and maintain loan records.
Conduct inspections or investigations of establishments for compliance with laws and regulations, such as those regarding health, quality, and safety.
Compile information about new accounts, enter account information into computers, and file related forms or other documents.
Inform customers of procedures for applying for services, such as ATM cards, direct deposit of checks, and certificates of deposit.
Collect and record customer deposits and fees, issue receipts, and investigate errors upon customers' request according to records.
Correspond with customers and confer with coworkers to answer inquiries or resolve account problems.
Prepare forms such as receipts, withdrawal orders, and transmittal papers based on transaction requests from stockholders.
Receive and respond to customer complaints and inform customers of order information such as unit prices and shipping dates.
Obtain customers' names and billing information, check inventory records, and verify order information.
Inspect outgoing work for compliance with customers' specifications and gather records pertinent to specific problems for review.
Prepare documents and correspondence such as damage claims, credit inquiries, invoices, and maintain files and control records for correspondence activities.
Route correspondence to other departments for reply and compose letters in response to customer inquiries regarding requests or billing issues.

Develop guidelines for nondiscriminatory employment practices, such as affirmative action plans and equal opportunity employment policies.
